page: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with 16 x86_64 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores using AMD EPYC 7543 processors clocked at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, and the architecture includes 16 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 64 KB of L1d cache. The system features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. Storage is bundled with a 1280 GB local SSD, and the configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2993.48, placing it in the top 25 percent of tested systems. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 166.8 GB/sec, alongside a strong large-block memory write bandwidth of 96.49 GB/sec. However, multi-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 47789.69 and Geekbench multi-core scores of 11156.0 fall within the average range. Memory read bandwidth, Redis SET operations at 29.54 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput at 15.34 GB/sec also perform at average levels.

The shared CPU allocation presents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, non-burstable compute capacity. Qualitatively, the server offers balanced resource density, combining a high-capacity 1280 GB SSD with a standard 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio. This makes it cost-efficient for workloads that benefit from local storage capacity without requiring dedicated CPU cores. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data decompression, static web serving, database operations, and moderate-scale application hosting.
description: The UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor, featuring 16 shared x86_64 vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 7543 processors at 2.0 GHz. It includes 64.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 1280 GB local SSD. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and large-block memory write bandwidth, while multi-core CPU and memory read metrics remain in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for sustained compute-heavy tasks, but the generous local SSD storage provides solid qualitative value for balanced workloads. This server is suited for database operations, multi-threaded decompression, static web serving, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose KVM server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 1280 GB SSD, delivering strong single-core performance and average multi-core benchmarks.
meta_description: UpCloud 16xCPU-64GB is a general-purpose KVM virtual server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a bundled 1280 GB local SSD.
tagline: A balanced virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processors, 64 GB RAM, and a high-capacity local SSD for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7543 vCPUs and 64 GB of system memory
Strong single-core CPU and large-block memory write performance
Average multi-core CPU and memory read benchmark results
Suited for database operations, web serving, and data decompression
